verses.1: Woe to her who is rebellious and defiled, The oppressive city!
verses.2: She obeyed no voice, She accepted no discipline. She did not trust in the LORD, She did not approach her God.
verses.3: Her leaders within her are roaring lions, Her judges are wolves at evening; They have no bones to gnaw in the morning.
verses.4: Her prophets are insolent, treacherous men; Her priests have profaned the sanctuary. They have done violence to the Law.
verses.5: The LORD is righteous within her; He will do no injustice. Every morning He brings His justice to light; He does not fail. But the criminal knows no shame.
verses.6: “I have eliminated nations; Their corner towers are deserted. I have laid waste their streets, With no one passing by; Their cities have been laid waste, Without a person, without an inhabitant.
verses.7: “I said, ‘You will certainly revere Me, You will accept discipline.’ So her dwelling will not be eliminated In accordance with everything that I have stipulated for her. Instead, they were eager to corrupt all their deeds.
verses.8: “Therefore wait for Me,” declares the LORD, “For the day when I rise up as a witness. Indeed, My decision is to gather nations, To assemble kingdoms, To pour out on them My indignation, All My burning anger; For all the earth will be devoured By the fire of My zeal.
verses.9: “For then I will restore to the peoples pure lips, So that all of them may call on the name of the LORD, To serve Him shoulder to shoulder.
verses.10: “From beyond the rivers of Ethiopia My worshipers, My dispersed ones, Will bring My offerings.
verses.11: “On that day you will feel no shame Because of all your deeds By which you have rebelled against Me; For then I will remove from your midst Your proud, arrogant ones, And you will never again be haughty On My holy mountain.
verses.12: “But I will leave among you A humble and lowly people, And they will take refuge in the name of the LORD.
verses.13: “The remnant of Israel will do no wrong And tell no lies, Nor will a deceitful tongue Be found in their mouths; For they will feed and lie down With no one to frighten them. ”
verses.14: Shout for joy, daughter of Zion! Shout in triumph, Israel! Rejoice and triumph with all your heart, Daughter of Jerusalem!
verses.15: The LORD has taken away His judgments against you, He has cleared away your enemies. The King of Israel, the LORD, is in your midst; You will no longer fear disaster.
verses.16: On that day it will be said to Jerusalem: “Do not be afraid, Zion; Do not let your hands fall limp.
verses.17: “The LORD your God is in your midst, A victorious warrior. He will rejoice over you with joy, He will be quiet in His love, He will rejoice over you with shouts of joy.
verses.18: “I will gather those who are worried about the appointed feasts— They came from you, Zion; The disgrace of exile is a burden on them.
verses.19: “Behold, I am going to deal at that time With all your oppressors; I will save those who limp And gather the scattered, And I will turn their shame into praise and fame In all the earth.
verses.20: “At that time I will bring you in, Even at the time when I gather you together; Indeed, I will make you famous and praiseworthy Among all the peoples of the earth, When I restore your fortunes before your eyes,” Says the LORD.
